title: oxalis depressa
intro: Oxalis depressa, also known as the small-flowered wood-sorrel, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Oxalis genus, characterized by its small, white flowers and edible leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to the presence of ascorbic acid and other flavonoids. Oxalis depressa has been associated with several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and providing antimicrobial properties, making it a popular herb in herbal remedies. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant can be consumed as a tea, used in salads, or applied topically in the form of a salve or ointment, although its preparations and uses should be done under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
